name: Chicken Breasts Stuffed with Pesto Butter
recipeIngredient: 6 boneless chicken breasts, Flattened to 1/8 inch
1/2 cup fresh basil leaves, washed and dried
1/3 cup all-purpose flour seasoned with salt and pepper
recipeInstructions:
Cooking Day Instructions:
1. Flatten chicken breasts to 1/8 inch thickness.
2. In a food processor or blender, combine basil, cheese, and garlic. Mix until a thick paste is formed.
3. Add butter and blend.
4. Shape into a 3-inch square and chill until firm (quick chill by placing in freezer for 10-20 minutes).
5. Slice chilled square into sticks, approximately 1/2-inch wide and three inches long.
6. Lay out the flattened chicken breasts. Place a stick of pesto butter on the larger side of the meat. Fold the remaining side over the butter, then fold under the two ends. Press meat together on all sides to securely enclose the butter.
7. Using 3 separate plates, place seasoned flour in one, beaten egg in another, and bread crumbs in the third.
8. Dredge each rolled chicken breast in flour, shaking off excess, then dip into egg, and roll in bread crumbs. Firmly pat bread crumbs onto chicken.
9. Refrigerate chicken rolls for at least 1 hour to allow breadcrumbs to adhere. Chicken rolls may be frozen after this step. Wrap each chicken roll in plastic wrap and place in freezer bags.
Serving Day Instructions:
1. Unwrap chicken rolls and put on a plate in the refrigerator to thaw completely.
2. In a large saute pan, heat butter and olive oil over medium-high heat.
3. Saute chicken for about 10-15 minutes, turning until golden brown on all sides.
4. Place chicken in a lightly greased baking dish and bake at 350°F (175°C) for approximately 40-45 minutes, or until meat is white throughout. Be careful not to overbake.
